# barndoor_llm_model_access (Resource)

Manages an LLM Gateway model-access policy: an allowlist or denylist of models and providers, applied to an identity scope (the organization, a user, an IdP group, …).

`scope_type`/`scope_id`/`scope_value` answer *who* the policy applies to; the `targets` list answers *what* is allowed or denied and is OR-ed when matching. A cleared `scope_id` or `scope_value` cannot be unset through the update API, so removing one forces a new policy.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Org-wide allowlist: only gpt-* aliases may be called.
resource "barndoor_llm_model_access" "frontier_only" {
  name        = "Frontier models only"
  scope_type  = "org"
  policy_type = "allowlist"

  targets = [
    { kind = "model_alias", alias = "gpt-*" },
  ]
}

# Deny a specific upstream model and everything on one provider for an
# IdP group, across both traffic lanes.
resource "barndoor_llm_model_access" "engineering_denylist" {
  name        = "Engineering denylist"
  scope_type  = "group"
  scope_value = "engineering"
  policy_type = "denylist"

  targets = [
    { kind = "model", model = "gpt-4o-mini" },
    { kind = "provider", provider_id = barndoor_llm_provider.staging.id },
  ]

  traffic_type = "all"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Human-readable policy name.
- `policy_type` (String) `allowlist` (only the targets are permitted) or `denylist` (the targets are blocked).
- `scope_type` (String) Identity dimension the policy applies to: one of `org` / `team` / `user` / `project` / `api_key` / `mcp_server` / `agent` / `role` / `group`. `org` applies to everyone; the others select by `scope_id` (UUID-keyed scopes) or `scope_value` (name-keyed scopes such as `role`/`group`).
- `targets` (Attributes List) What the policy allows or denies; entries are OR-ed when matching. At least one is required. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--targets))

### Optional

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the policy is enforced. Defaults to `true`.
- `scope_id` (String) UUID of the scoped entity (user, team, API key, …) for UUID-keyed scope types. Removing it forces a new resource — the API cannot clear it in place.
- `scope_value` (String) String key of the scoped entity for name-keyed scope types (e.g. a role or IdP group name for `role`/`group` scopes). Removing it forces a new resource — the API cannot clear it in place.
- `traffic_type` (String) Traffic lane the policy applies to: `all`, `llm` (model-proxy traffic), or `mcp` (MCP tool traffic). Defaults to `llm`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Policy UUID assigned by the API; also the `terraform import` key.
- `org_id` (String) Organization the policy belongs to, resolved from the provider credential's token claims.

<a id="nestedatt--targets"></a>
### Nested Schema for `targets`

Required:

- `kind` (String) Target dimension: `model_alias` (matches the caller-facing alias; requires `alias`), `model` (matches the resolved upstream model; requires `model`), `provider` (any model on the provider; requires `provider_id`), or `provider_model` (an alias on a specific provider; requires `provider_id` and `model`).

Optional:

- `alias` (String) Caller-facing model alias to match, with an optional trailing `*` wildcard (`model_alias` targets).
- `model` (String) Model name to match, with an optional trailing `*` wildcard: the resolved upstream model for `model` targets, the caller-facing alias for `provider_model` targets.
- `provider_id` (String) UUID of the `barndoor_llm_provider` to match (`provider` and `provider_model` targets).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by policy UUID.
terraform import barndoor_llm_model_access.frontier_only 9d3e7f5a-2b3c-4d5e-9f0a-1b2c3d4e5f6a
```
